data.description:
# S4 â€” RFC 7662 token-introspection verifier

## Why
Introspection completes the "validate against an external Authorization Server" story for
providers that issue **opaque** access tokens (or when instant revocation matters) â€” Keycloak,
Okta, Auth0, WorkOS all expose a standard `/introspect` endpoint. It is the pattern the SDK's own
`simple-auth` example demonstrates.

## What
Add an **`IntrospectionTokenVerifier`** to `src/rebar/_mcp_auth.py`, registered on the composite
seam, selected via `mcp.auth.strategies` with params `introspection_endpoint`, `client_id`,
`client_secret`, `audience`, optional positive-cache `ttl`. It POSTs the token to the endpoint
(RFC 7662) with client authentication, accepts only `active: true` with a matching audience, and
maps `scope`/`exp`/`sub`/`client_id` into an `AccessToken`.

## Approach & rationale
Model on the SDK's shipped `IntrospectionTokenVerifier` example (the blessed reference) but add
the production hardening its docstring says it lacks: client authentication on the introspection
call, a short positive-only cache, and strict audience checking. Reuse the already-present `httpx`.

## Scope
The introspection verifier only, on the S2 seam. Subset of the epic. Depends on S2. Independent of
S3/S5/S6.

## Security
Boundary-crossing. Requirements: call the introspection endpoint over authenticated TLS
(`client_secret_basic`), HTTPS-only endpoint (SSRF guard), bounded timeout; accept only
`active:true` with a matching `aud` (RFC 8707); **fail-closed** on non-200/timeout/malformed
(never treat an unreachable AS as allow); cache **positives only** with a TTL bounded by the
token `exp` (never cache failures); never log the token.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test (tier: unit against the real verifier; irreducible boundary â€” the introspection
      HTTP call â€” is a stateful fake fed production-shaped RFC 7662 responses) asserts an
      `active:true` response with a matching audience â†’ an `AccessToken` with the expected scopes.
- [ ] Unit tests assert rejection (None) for: `active:false`; a non-200 response; a timeout/HTTP
      error (fail-closed); and a mismatched `aud` â€” each a distinct negative control.
- [ ] Unit test asserts positive results are cached for at most the configured TTL and never
      beyond `exp`, and that a failure is not cached (observable oracle: second call within TTL
      makes no HTTP call; a prior failure does).
- [ ] Unit test asserts the introspection request carries client authentication and targets only
      an HTTPS endpoint (observable oracle: the fake receives Basic auth; an http:// endpoint is
      rejected at construction).
- [ ] Integration test (httpx `ASGITransport`) asserts an introspection-validated token is
      accepted (200) end-to-end.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

## Compatibility
Additive verifier, opt-in via `mcp.auth.strategies`. No change to other verifiers or stdio.
